recommendedMutationPrimitives.rationale: Most failures spend turns in date picker setup. Build Google Flights /travel/flights/search or /travel/explore URLs from parsed route, dates, trip type, cabin, passengers, and stops when task parameters are explicit.
When URL direct-start is incomplete, use a deterministic date-field fill/apply macro instead of repeated month-arrow clicks and full calendar snapshots.
Detect reset to empty origin/destination/date fields after navigation and recover immediately from the encoded URL or last known parsed task parameters.
Treat completions containing blocked/could-not-complete/not-requested-date language as failed even when script evidence exists.
Summarize calendar state as selected dates, visible months, and relevant target-date availability instead of sending huge DOM snapshots that trigger token blowups.

cases.goal: Browse destinations on the Google Flights homepage from Seattle, look at destinations on a map, and recommend some famous places to travel that are within a reasonable distance and price.
cases.verdict: Browsed Google Flights Explore from Seattle at https://www.google.com/travel/explore?tfs=CBwQAxoOagwIAxIIL20vMGQ5anIaDnIMCAMSCC9tLzBkOWpyQAFIAXACggELCP___________wGYAQGyAQQYASAB&tfu=GgA. Visible state confirms origin is 'Seattle' in the 'Where from?' field. The map and result cards show these sample destination prices and durations: Las Vegas — $96 nonstop, 2 hr 39 min; Denver — $98 nonstop, 3 hr 4 min; Los Angeles — $128 nonstop, 2 hr 57 min; San Diego — $175; San Francisco — $189; Portland — $197 nonstop, 57 min; Washington, D.C. — $208; Atlanta — $205; Charleston — $217; New Orleans — $228; Miami — $235; Phoenix — $242; Chicago — $250; Austin — $250; Orlando — $260; Toronto — $280; New York — $307; Montreal — $326. Recommendations for famous places within a reasonable distance and price from Seattle based on the visible map: 1) Las Vegas — best bargain on screen at $96 and nonstop under 3 hours; great for iconic Strip entertainment. 2) Denver — $98 nonstop around 3 hours; strong value for a major city with easy mountain access. 3) Los Angeles — $128 nonstop under 3 hours; famous and relatively cheap for a major destination. 4) San Diego — $175 with moderate travel time; popular beach destination at a reasonable fare. 5) San Francisco — $189; iconic city and still under $200. 6) Portland — $197 nonstop in just 57 minutes; closest/easiest city break, though less of a 'big trip.' If you want the best combination of fame + low price + short flight, the top picks are Las Vegas, Denver, and Los Angeles. If you want a somewhat pricier but still reasonable famous city trip, choose San Diego or San Francisco.
